Starburst Features 2026: The Complete Guide for South African Players

If you’ve spent any time exploring online slots in South Africa, chances are you’ve already come across Starburst. NetEnt’s most iconic game has been pulling players back since 2012 — and in 2026, it still holds its own against titles released last month. The question most players ask isn’t whether to play it, but whether they actually understand how everything works under the bonnet.

This guide breaks down every Starburst special feature in exhaustive detail — from the iconic expanding wilds to the win-both-ways mechanic, the paylines structure, and what to expect from the newer versions like Starburst XXXtreme and Starburst Galaxy. We’ll also tackle the question doing the rounds in SA casino communities: what is the Starburst Challenge?

Whether you’re a complete beginner or someone who’s been spinning those gemstone reels for years, you’ll walk away from this page understanding Starburst better than before.

Starburst Features Overview 2026

Before we get into the weeds, let’s lay out the full feature set at a glance. Starburst is deliberately lean — and that’s actually one of its greatest strengths.

FeatureDetails
ProviderNetEnt (Evolution Gaming group)
Release Year2012
RTP96.09%
VolatilityLow
Paylines10 fixed (effectively 20 with Win Both Ways)
Reels5 x 3 grid
Min BetR1.80
Max BetR1,800
Max Win500x your stake
Wild TypeExpanding Wild (reels 2, 3, 4)
Free SpinsNo — but re-spins triggered by wilds
Bonus RoundNo
Scatter SymbolNo
Progressive JackpotNo
Hit Frequency22.6%

Simple? On the surface, absolutely. But Starburst slot gameplay has layers most casual players never fully appreciate. Let’s dig in.

Core Mechanics Deep Dive

Starburst Expanding Wilds: The Star of the Show

The Starburst Wild isn’t just a standard substitution symbol — it’s the entire engine driving this game’s excitement.

Here’s exactly how it works.

What it looks like: A multicoloured, jewel-studded star that radiates light outward across the reel. Impossible to miss.

Where it lands: The Starburst Wild can only appear on reels 2, 3, and 4. Never on reel 1 or reel 5. This is intentional — the middle reels are positioned to generate the most payline coverage when a full-reel wild locks in.

What happens when it lands:

  1. The wild expands instantly to fill the entire reel — all three rows become wild
  2. A re-spin triggers at no cost to you
  3. The expanded wild reel locks in place while the remaining reels spin again
  4. If another wild lands on a different eligible reel during the re-spin, it also expands and locks — triggering yet another re-spin
  5. This can chain for a maximum of three consecutive re-spins

The expanding wilds substitute for every other symbol on the grid. With two or three wild reels locked simultaneously, you’re looking at wins across multiple paylines — running in both directions, because of the Win Both Ways mechanic.

Maximum wild scenario: All three middle reels — 2, 3, and 4 — fill with expanding wilds simultaneously. Combined with Win Both Ways, this is where the 500x maximum payout lives.

Re-spin chain in practice:

  • Wild lands on reel 3 → reel 3 locks, re-spin triggers
  • During re-spin, wild lands on reel 2 → reel 2 locks, another re-spin triggers
  • During second re-spin, wild lands on reel 4 → reel 4 locks, final re-spin triggers
  • Third re-spin plays out with three wild reels in place

Three re-spins is the absolute ceiling. After the third completes, win totals are calculated and the round ends.

Win Both Ways: Double Your Payline Power

With Starburst’s win both ways mechanic, a matching combination of three or more symbols pays out regardless of which direction it runs across the payline. Left to right, right to left — both count independently.

A practical example:

  • Three Bar symbols on reels 3, 4, and 5 → pays right to left
  • Three gems on reels 1, 2, and 3 → pays left to right
  • Both wins calculated and paid on the same spin

This mechanic effectively turns the 10 fixed paylines into 20 active win opportunities per spin. Combined with the expanding wilds, Win Both Ways is what gives the game its surprisingly strong hit frequency of 22.6%.

Starburst Paylines and Symbols Explained

The Payline Structure

Starburst runs 10 fixed paylines across a 5-reel, 3-row grid. You cannot deactivate any of them — always on, every spin. Full payline coverage guaranteed, no accidentally missed winning combinations.

The Symbol Hierarchy

High-Paying Symbols:

  • Bar — The single best symbol in the game. Five of a kind pays 250 coins at Bet Level 1.
  • Red Lucky 7 — Second strongest. Five of a kind returns 120 coins at base bet level.
  • Yellow Diamond Gem — Top tier among the gems, paying 60 coins for five of a kind.
  • Green Gem — Worth 50 coins for five of a kind.
  • Orange Gem — Returns 40 coins for five across a payline.

Low-Paying Symbols:

  • Blue Gem — 25 coins for five of a kind.
  • Purple Gem — Also 25 coins for five of a kind.

Wild Symbol:

  • Starburst Wild Star — No fixed coin value. Its worth comes from the combinations it creates.

Payouts above reflect Bet Level 1. Scale up to Level 10 and payouts increase sharply.

Special Features and Visual Effects

The game runs on a space-themed backdrop of swirling galaxies and luminous nebulae. Gemstone symbols glow with light on every spin. When a win lands, arcade-style light trails track the winning combination across the active paylines.

The soundtrack is a synth-heavy electronic composition — upbeat without becoming irritating over long sessions, which suits the low-volatility rhythm perfectly.

Starburst XXXtreme Features

Starburst XXXtreme dropped in 2021, nearly a decade after the original. It takes the core DNA of Starburst and injects high-octane risk for players chasing far bigger potential payouts.

FeatureOriginal StarburstStarburst XXXtreme
Release20122021
VolatilityLowHigh
RTP96.09%96.26%
Bonus FeaturesRe-spins onlyPaid triggers + random multipliers
Max Win500x stake200,000x stake
Best ForBeginners, casual playersHigh-rollers, risk-takers

XXXtreme offers a max win of 200,000x your stake versus the original’s 500x ceiling. It introduces multipliers attached to the expanding wilds, which can stack during re-spins. The paid feature trigger lets players buy direct access to the bonus round.

For South African players: if you want frequent, manageable wins that sustain a long session, stick with the classic. If you’re comfortable riding out dry spells for larger potential payouts, XXXtreme is the natural next step.

Starburst Galaxy Features

Starburst Galaxy is the third major entry in the franchise, representing NetEnt’s clearest attempt to bring Starburst into the modern era of feature-rich video slots. It addresses the most consistent criticism of the original — its deliberately minimal bonus structure — while sitting between the classic and XXXtreme in terms of risk profile.

For the most current information on Starburst Galaxy mechanics and availability at South African casinos, check the dedicated review page where we track confirmed updates as they arrive.

Starburst Slot Gameplay in Action

You’ve settled on R18 per spin. The reels turn and three purple gems land on payline four running left to right. Small win. Nothing dramatic. You spin again.

Then a wild star drops onto reel 3. The star expands, filling all three rows with that luminous rainbow pattern. Re-spin triggers. A second wild lands on reel 2. Expands. Another re-spin. Two full wild reels now in play.

The second re-spin plays out. No third wild — but with reels 2 and 3 fully wild across 10 paylines running in both directions, the win calculation lights up like a pinball machine. A modest R18 bet has returned R270.

That’s Starburst gameplay at its peak. The 22.6% hit frequency means quiet periods are part of the experience. But when the expanding wilds chain together — genuinely electric.

What Is the Starburst Challenge?

The Starburst Challenge is not an official NetEnt bonus feature. You won’t find it in the paytable or triggered during normal gameplay. It’s a community-driven concept spread across casino streamers, social media groups, and certain operator promotions.

In its most common form, players attempt to reach specific win targets — typically 100x or 500x their starting balance — using only Starburst within a set number of spins or a fixed budget. Some South African casinos have formalised this into promotional campaigns.

Be cautious about any casino presenting a “Starburst Challenge” as a formal game feature. It’s marketing language, not game mechanics. Read the full terms before participating.

How All the Features Work Together

The expanding wilds are powerful precisely because of Win Both Ways. A single wild reel covering reel 3 affects every payline in both directions simultaneously — potentially generating wins across eight or more paylines at the same time.

The fixed paylines remove complexity. Maximum coverage guaranteed on every spin, no manual configuration required.

Low volatility does the heavy lifting in between. Between wild re-spin events, small gem-matching wins sustain your session. Your balance stays healthy enough that when the expanding wilds finally chain together, you’ve got real money riding on the outcome.

The symbol hierarchy supports it all. Bar and 7 paying significantly more than the gems means that when high-value symbols complete combinations across expanded wild reels, the difference in outcome is real.

Comparison: Starburst Features vs Modern Slots

FeatureStarburst (2012)Modern Slot (2024–2026 average)
Wild TypeExpandingExpanding, Sticky, Multiplier, Walking
Free SpinsNo (re-spins only)Standard
Bonus RoundNoUsually yes
Scatter SymbolNoUsually yes
MultipliersNoCommon
Max Win500x5,000x–25,000x+
Progressive JackpotNoOften available
VolatilityLowUsually medium-high
Hit Frequency22.6%Typically lower

Where Starburst wins is clear: a hit frequency of 22.6% is genuinely high for any slot. The RTP of 96.09% sits above industry average. For South African players managing modest bankrolls, Starburst remains one of the most bankroll-friendly options available.

Tips for Getting the Most Out of Starburst Features

1. Understand what volatility means for your session Starburst is low volatility — consistent smaller wins punctuated by occasional larger wild-chain payouts. Play for sustainable sessions, not single-spin jackpots.

2. Accept the fixed paylines Full payline coverage on every spin, no exceptions. You’ll never watch a winning combination land on a deactivated line.

3. Size your bet to your session length The betting range runs from R1.80 to R1,800 per spin, giving South African players real flexibility. Long session? Lower your bet. Short, high-intensity play? Scale up.

4. Don’t chase the expanding wilds The RNG determines every outcome independently. Increasing your bet after a dry spell does nothing for your odds. Gambler’s fallacy. Stick to your planned bet size.

5. Use demo mode first Most South African casinos offer a free Starburst demo. Use it to watch how the re-spin chain unfolds before committing real money.

6. Set your session limit before you load the game Decide your budget and time limit before the first spin, not halfway through.

7. Know when to try Starburst XXXtreme If you’ve played the classic extensively and want bigger swings, XXXtreme is the natural next step. Be ready for longer losing stretches between significant wins.

FAQ: Starburst Features Explained

Q: Does Starburst have free spins? Not in the traditional sense. There’s no free spins bonus round triggered by scatter symbols. What it has is a re-spin feature — triggered when the expanding wild lands on reels 2, 3, or 4. It can chain up to three consecutive re-spins at no cost to you.

Q: How many paylines does Starburst have? Ten fixed paylines. Because of Win Both Ways, those 10 paylines evaluate winning combinations in both directions — giving you 20 active win opportunities on every spin.

Q: Can the Starburst Wild appear on every reel? No. The expanding wild only lands on reels 2, 3, and 4. Reels 1 and 5 are wild-free.

Q: What is the maximum win on Starburst? 500x your total stake per spin. At maximum bet (R1,800 per spin), that’s a potential R900,000 payout from a single spin.

Q: What makes Starburst different from Starburst XXXtreme? The original: low volatility, 500x max win, re-spins only. XXXtreme: high volatility, 200,000x max win, random multipliers on wilds, paid feature buy. Same expanding wild concept — completely different risk profiles.

Q: Is there a scatter symbol in Starburst? No. The Starburst Wild is the only bonus-triggering symbol. The absence of scatters is one of the things that makes Starburst genuinely distinct.

Q: What is the RTP for Starburst? Standard RTP is 96.09%, above industry average. In some markets, NetEnt offers a variant reaching 99.06% — check the in-game rules at your specific casino for the applicable figure.

Q: Is Starburst suitable for beginners? Absolutely. Low volatility, simple mechanics, fixed paylines, clear symbol hierarchy — Starburst is one of the most consistently recommended first slots for new players.

Q: Can I play Starburst on mobile in South Africa? Yes, fully. Starburst runs on any modern Android or iOS browser — no download required. The bold, high-contrast visuals work particularly well on smartphone screens.

Q: What is the Starburst Challenge? A community and promotional concept, not an official game feature. It typically involves reaching a set win target within a fixed number of spins or a specific budget. Read the full terms before participating in any casino promotion using this name.

Where to Play Starburst in South Africa

Fourteen years after its release, Starburst still ranks in the top ten at most online casinos globally. For South African players, it remains one of the most accessible, fair, and genuinely enjoyable options available — and that’s not nostalgia talking.

The expanding wilds remain as satisfying as they were on day one. Win Both Ways doubles effective payline coverage without adding complexity. And the overall gameplay strikes a balance between simplicity and depth that modern slots frequently attempt — and rarely manage to nail.
